Resilience in the Desert

Walker Lake & Bighorn Sheep — around twenty artists gather for a desert lake the state is letting disappear.

Free admission All July In connection with Artown

The poster
Walker Lake
Stained glass
Jeweled Bighorn

The Cause

Since 1882, Walker Lake has given up more than 160 feet of itself — its river drawn off upstream, year after year, until the fish went first, then the birds that came for them. Yet the Pai­ute tell of Tawaga, the serpent that dwells there still: the lake was never only water. This July, around twenty artists hold that presence up to the light. Resilience is not resistance — it is finding beauty in what may be dying.

Curated by Micky Kim · The Bighorn Crossing
